## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On October 21, 1805, the incredibly massive, historically definitive, and absolutely spectacular naval Battle of Trafalgar aggressively occurred. The massive British Royal Navy, heavily commanded by the legendary Admiral Lord Nelson, completely and violently annihilated the combined fleets of the French and Spanish Navies off the coast of Spain. This massive, aggressive victory absolutely shattered Napoleon Bonaparte's massive plans to invade Great Britain, fiercely cementing the absolute, unassailable global naval supremacy of the British Empire for over a century.

On October 21, 1966, the incredibly massive, tragically horrific, and utterly devastating Aberfan disaster aggressively struck Wales. A massive colliery spoil tip, heavily loosened by days of intense rain, violently collapsed and aggressively slid down a massive mountainside. The incredibly massive avalanche of coal waste completely engulfed the Pantglas Junior School and a row of houses, tragically crushing and killing 116 children and 28 adults, deeply traumatizing the entire massive United Kingdom and forcing incredibly fierce overhauls of massive mining safety regulations.
